stories.title: EU Parliament chief seeks probe into Russia-friendly lawmaker
stories.summary: STRASBOURG — European Parliament President Roberta Metsola wants the lawmakers’ oversight body to assess whether Luxembourg MEP Fernand Kartheiser breached the code of conduct by meeting with Russian officials, according to a letter seen by POLITICO. Kartheiser — who was kicked out of the European Conservatives and Reformists group in the Parliament last year over a previous trip to Moscow — has held at least four video calls with members of the Russian Duma and has traveled to the country on tw

stories.title: Romanian PM-designate loses party support as political crisis deepens
stories.summary: Romania’s latest prime minister-designate failed to secure the backing of his own party on Tuesday, impeding the path to forming a new government. Adrian Veștea — a former mayor, county council president and development minister from the center-right National Liberal Party (PLN) — was announced on Sunday as Romanian President Nicușor Dan’s second prime minister-designate in two weeks, after previous candidate Eugen Tomac failed to win enough support to form a technocratic government. But o

stories.title: RFK Jr under fire for ‘bullying’ letter to scientific journal
stories.summary: Health advocates criticized Kennedy’s move demanding answers from journal that removed ‘flawed’ vaccine study Robert F Kennedy Jr , the US health secretary, is demanding answers from a medical journal that recently removed a paper suggesting a link between vaccines and infant death, saying their decision was “of great interest to me”. Public health advocates immediately criticized the move, and said Kennedy appeared to be trying to intimidate and influence the journal’s editorial process. The jo

stories.title: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
stories.summary: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

stories.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
stories.summary: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

stories.title: Az unió nem akarta hátrahagyni Magyarországot, csak nem oda mentek a pénzek, ahova kellett volna – az EU-s pénzekről vitázik az Országgyűlés
stories.summary: Nagy Imre 1989-es újratemetésének napjáról is megemlékeztek a képviselők. Magyar Péter szerint Orbán Viktor aprópénzre váltotta az 1989-es történelmi beszédét.

stories.title: Otthon, természet és harmónia Budán – ideális lokáció a XI. kerületben
stories.summary: Budapesten kevés olyan hely maradt, ahol egyszerre lehet hallani madarakat és akár felpattanni a villamosra a közeli városközpont felé – a Kána-tó környéke viszont ez a környék. Az elmúlt években egyre többen fedezték fel a városnak ezt a részét, ahol néhány perc alatt át lehet jutni a bevásárlóközpontok, irodák és közlekedési csomópontok közeléből a nádasok és tóparti séták világába.

stories.title: Új vezetők és új szerep: gyökeresen át kell alakítani az MFB-t, hogy alkalmas legyen az uniós pénz fogadására
stories.summary: Áll a bál a Magyar Fejlesztési Banknál. Érkeznek az új vezetők, a régi programok folyósítása megállt, átvilágítások kezdődnek. Minderre a kormánynak jó oka van: az intézmény kulcsszerepet tölt be abban, hogy idejében le lehessen hívni az RRF-pénzeket.

stories.summary: Government urged to reconsider proposal for 1,100 Afghans, currently stranded in Qatar, who worked with US forces Dozens of US lawmakers urged the Trump administration on Thursday to roll back any plans to ship to unsafe third countries Afghan nationals who worked with US forces during the war in their homeland. stories.title: Will life improve for Hungary's Roma community under Magyar?
stories.summary: Under Viktor Orban, the Roma community was kept at the very bottom of the social ladder. His successor is taking a stand against antiziganism. But can Hungarian Roma expect fundamental changes?

stories.summary: Leaders urge Donald Trump to stand behind Ukraine and pressure Putin to negotiate; Russia allows sale of substandard fuel amid supply crisis. stories.title: Sweden votes to back laws reinforcing its immigration crackdown
stories.summary: So-called ‘good behaviour’ legislation fiercely criticised by opposition politicians and rights groups Sweden’s parliament has voted to escalate the country’s crackdown on immigrant rights, backing laws that allow authorities to revoke residency permits based on a vague criteria of bad behaviour and obliging most public sector workers to report anyone suspected of being undocumented. The new legislation comes ahead of parliamentary elections in September, pitting the centre-right government, whi

stories.title: Trump promised to bring order to AI oversight. That lasted 2 weeks.
stories.summary: The White House’s last-minute restrictions on Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence model are raising fundamental doubts about President Donald Trump’s 2-week-old effort to place guardrails on the technology. And they’re undercutting the president’s promise of a light-touch approach to regulating advanced AI, according to tech industry representatives, policy experts and at least one administration official. The June 2 order was meant to set up a process that could address the technology’s cyb

stories.title: Wannabe despot, dashing diplomat or boring back-office swot? Greece’s founding father divides opinion
stories.summary: He built modern Greece from the ground up, but Ioannis Kapodistrias remains a controversial figure. A new biopic throws light on this overlooked titan of European history On a hilltop in central Corfu, a marble bust carved in the classical style gazes skyward, lean, fine-featured and composed to the point of austerity. There is no uniform, no decorations, nor symbols of office, just a name cut into the base in Greek capitals: Ι Α ΚΑΠΟΔΙΣΤΡΙΑΣ. The bust stands alone in the gardens of Koukouritsa,

stories.summary: Few analysts believe final settlement can be reached in 60 days – and even if it is, war and instability could soon return In much of the Middle East, news that the US and Iran had come to a fragile agreement was greeted with relief tempered with doubt that any deal would resolve the turbulent region’s deep problems or even prevent a future return to war. stories.title: If the UK wants to rejoin the European Union, it first needs to understand it | Timothy Garton Ash
stories.summary: A full return will require a marathon of democratic persuasion, on the continent as much as in Britain. For one side holds most of the cards As Britain approaches the 10th anniversary of its vote to leave the EU, the British are beginning to debate rejoining what they call Europe. But, as in most previous British debates about “Europe”, this is Europe with the Europe left out. The discussion is all about what would be best for Britain economically and the British politics of getting there. Littl

stories.title: French spy service drops Palantir
stories.summary: PARIS — France’s domestic intelligence service is ending its contract with U.S. surveillance technology giant Palantir, it said on Tuesday morning. The General Directorate for Internal Security (DGSI) picked French firm ChapsVision to “replace the American giant Palantir,” Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said in an online video published on the eve of the opening of the country’s biggest tech conference VivaTech. stories.title: Dutch children are unusually happy and healthy. Is it because of this walking ritual?
stories.summary: Once a year, Dutch kids, parents and teachers take part in a walking festival, heading out for four nights in a single week to explore their neighbourhoods, exercise and make friends. It’s a tradition that seems to be genuinely transformative I shouldn’t have been surprised that the rain didn’t stop the Dutch kids. stories.title: Macron, Zelenskyy heard on camera game-planning how to handle Trump
stories.summary: EVIAN-LES-BAINS, France — Just like Ringo, Volodymyr Zelenskyy gets by with a little help from his friends. Upon his arrival in Evian-les-Bains at the G7 leaders’ summit on Tuesday, Zelenskyy was overheard on camera with French President Emmanuel Macron game-planning on how to deal with U.S. President Donald Trump. stories.summary: Senate Republicans say there are many unanswered questions about the deal and question inclusion of funds for Iran The US vice-president, ⁠JD Vance, has said a number of issues still need to be figured out with Iran, as many Republicans expressed scepticism of the agreement reached this week by Donald Trump, and pressed the White House to release more information about it. stories.summary: Brian Gu says he sees Chinese car firms competing on quality rather than launching price war as at home Motorists in the UK and EU should not expect a sharp drop in the cost of electric vehicles despite increased competition among Chinese manufacturers, one of the country’s biggest electric carmakers has said. stories.title: Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says
stories.summary: Almost every child, including those from high-income countries, is now exposed to at least one hazard Half of the world’s children are exposed to at least three overlapping climate hazards threatening their health, education and survival, according to a Unicef report. Globally, children face increasing threats from heatwaves, storms, floods and droughts as the climate crisis worsens, with more than one billion facing at least three of these at once. stories.title: Spanish households save €10 a month thanks to renewables expansion, report finds
stories.summary: Thinktank says decoupling electricity from gas prices has also helped shield Spain from hikes caused by Iran war Spanish households save €10 a month on electricity bills because of wind turbines and solar panels installed in the last five years, a report has found. Typical energy bills would be 19% more expensive if electricity costs were still as tightly coupled to gas prices as in 2021, according to Ember, a climate thinktank. stories.title: UK ministers lobby Trump to avert backlash against social media ban

stories.title: Kingsmill owner cleared to create UK’s biggest bread brand with Hovis takeover

stories.title: Trump facing pressure to condemn UFC fighter for disparaging Michelle Obama

stories.title: Thirst review – member-dismembering Icelandic gore fest rips it up in trashy 80s style
stories.summary: A 1,000-year-old vampire obsessed with removing men’s genitals is the main storyline in this body horror, filmed in trashy 1980s synth-heavy style Wibbling willies! This gore fest from Iceland starts as it means to go on: parked on a quiet back road, where a balding 1,000-year-old vampire has lured a middle-aged man into his car with the promise of a quickie. stories.title: I’ve found a way to get free ferries to France – and I can’t believe my luck | Zoe Williams
stories.summary: While taking the boat to Dunkirk, I stumbled upon a remarkable deal. I’ve barely been on dry land since In February, my sister and I took the ferry from Dover to Dunkirk. On the way back, I noticed there was a deal: spend €150 (£130) in duty free and get a free day-trip ticket. If you love Toblerone and know anyone who smokes, you can spend that without even blinking – and so it came to pass that I was back on the ferry within a month. stories.title: Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%

stories.title: ‘Russia should make a deal,’ Trump says after ‘very good meeting’ with Zelenskyy at G7 – Europe live
stories.summary: US president confirms he will meet with Ukrainian leader again later today after today’s earlier G7 meeting … and given the delay this morning, the meeting may or may not have happened already – guess we will find out at some point during the day. in Évian-les-Bains Continue reading...

stories.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
stories.summary: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977 The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief. Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential pa

stories.title: Iran updates: Netanyahu refrains from criticizing Iran deal
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu did say he doesn't always share the same views as US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: California Governor Newsom says being investigated by Trump's administration
stories.summary: Trump's political rival Gavin Newsom said that Trump is targeting him and his wife by investigating them, without specifying the allegations. The California governor has locked horns with Trump on several occasions.

stories.title: G7 summit: Ukraine and Iran head agenda
stories.summary: The heads of the world's richest nations are gathering in France this week. Russia's war on Ukraine, along with the peace process with Iran, will be top topics. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Indonesia hit by 6.7 magnitude earthquake
stories.summary: The earthquake hit part of Indonesia's Sulawesi island. Indonesia lies on the Pacific "Ring of Fire" and is therefore prone to frequent, strong earthquakes.

stories.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
stories.summary: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

stories.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
stories.summary: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

stories.title: India blocks Telegram before a nationwide medical college entrance exam
stories.summary: The Indian government said Telegram was used to "defraud candidates" taking the NEET medical entrance exam. The messaging app, which is very popular in the country, is due to be unblocked after the exam has taken place.

stories.title: US-Iran deal: Tehran says Israel withdrawal from Lebanon key
stories.summary: Iran’s top diplomat says any continued Israeli presence in Lebanon would violate the interim U.S.–Iran deal to end the war. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu insists Israel will remain as long as necessary. DW has more.

stories.title: Sigh of relief in Africa as the Strait of Hormuz 'reopens'
stories.summary: A potential US-Iran agreement could lower energy-, fertilizer- and food prices in Africa if the Strait of Hormuz fully reopens. Oil exporters such as Nigeria and Angola might have to cope with less revenue, experts say.

stories.title: World Cup: Cape Verde hero and Iran suffer US travel woes
stories.summary: After starring in Cape Verde's draw with Spain, goalkeeper Vozinha shared his sadness that an expensive travel bond stopped his mother from attending. On the same day, Iran's coach said his team were being "oppressed."

stories.title: Germany news: Population shrinks for first time since COVID-induced dip in 2020
stories.summary: A dip in net migration meant it could not cancel out Germany's low birth rates in 2025. Meanwhile, fuel prices dip amid news of a possible deal between the US and Iran. And Merz gives Trump a Germany jersey. DW has more.

stories.title: California's Gavin Newsom alleges justice department is investigating his wife and ex-staff
stories.summary: A source familiar with the investigations says they had been ongoing for "roughly a year".

stories.title: A year on, six questions still haunt the Air India crash investigation
stories.summary: From fuel switches to engine failures, here are the biggest mysteries around the Air India crash inquiry.

stories.title: What did Trump do differently to Obama on Iran?
stories.summary: The BBC's Gary O'Donoghue looks at the contrasting approaches of the two presidents as Trump touts a new peace deal.

stories.title: Recovery of Ebola patients offers rare moments of joy at epicentre of outbreak
stories.summary: There are glimpses of happiness in the Democratic Republic of Congo's fight against the virus that has killed more than 170.

stories.title: Married at First Sight Australia allegations 'disturbing', says country's watchdog
stories.summary: The claims also prompted a response from UK media regulator Ofcom, who called them "deeply concerning".

stories.title: Australia to probe assault claims by Gaza flotilla activists against Israeli forces
stories.summary: Australian activists claim they were kidnapped, raped and tortured after being detained in May.

stories.title: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
stories.summary: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

stories.title: Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
stories.summary: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

stories.title: Trust in news hits a new low, research suggests
stories.summary: The Reuters Institute records its lowest trust figures since the annual research began in 2015.

stories.title: Trump appears hesitant about ally-proposed plan to demine Strait of Hormuz for commercial traffic
stories.summary: Trump appeared hesitant to accept the European proposal, stressing that the strategic waterway will become open and safe thanks to his tentative deal to end the war with Iran, but remained open to the idea of some naval presence to ensure water safety for commercial passage.

stories.title: Iranian-Americans protest against Iran team at World Cup
stories.summary: The BBC’s Shaimaa Khalil was outside the Iran v New Zealand opening round match as protesters called for an end to Tehran’s clerical regime.

stories.title: Iran 'played Trump like a violin' in deal, former national security adviser Bolton tells Euronews
stories.summary: Bolton tells Euronews the US sacrificed its strategic leverage as Trump prioritised lowering oil prices, while Iran walks away with the deal it wanted.

stories.title: Eight killed in US Air Force B-52 bomber crash in California
stories.summary: Eight people were killed when a B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in Southern California. Officials said the aircraft was on a routine test mission and the cause remains under investigation.

stories.title: Tunisia sack head coach just one game into World Cup
stories.summary: Tunisia sack Sabri Lamouchi one game into their World Cup, with former Morocco and Saudi Arabia manager Herve Renard replacing him.

stories.title: 'Dancing girl's' bare torso restored in Indian textbook after backlash
stories.summary: A picture in a new school textbook had covered up the naked torso of the famous figurine with dark shading.

stories.title: Crowds flock to JR's giant cave installation on Paris' Pont Neuf
stories.summary: Visitors queued to explore French artist JR's new immersive installation, which transforms Paris' oldest bridge, the Pont Neuf, into a giant cave open around the clock.

stories.title: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.title: Hundreds of cats destined for slaughter rescued in Vietnam as police dismantle theft ring
stories.summary: Vietnamese police arrested nine people and rescued more than 400 live cats destined for slaughter, reuniting dozens with their owners. Around 100 of the animals later died from their ordeal.

stories.title: Nigerian army frees widow of ex-general who died in captivity
stories.summary: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar and his wife Amina were abducted in north-west Nigeria at the end of last month.

stories.title: Ships start to trickle through Strait of Hormuz, but who will run it still in doubt
stories.summary: Trump said the strait would be "completely open" by Friday, but key details over who will manage the crucial waterway remain unresolved. Meanwhile, Iran has made further claims that it will control Hormuz in the future based on its interpretation of the framework deal with the US.

stories.title: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam paper leak concerns
stories.summary: The NEET exam had earlier been scrapped over allegations of a paper leak, sparking massive protests.

stories.title: Watch: California wildfires rage near passing vehicles
stories.summary: The fires in Riverside County cover over 2,000 acres, say authorities.

stories.title: US businessman detained in Myanmar over alleged Chamber of Commerce fraud
stories.summary: A former US Marine and ex-AmCham Myanmar president was detained at Yangon airport Thursday after the chamber uncovered $300,000 in allegedly unauthorised payments linked to former board members.

stories.title: La DGSI va remplacer Palantir par ChapsVision, annonce Sébastien Lecornu
stories.summary: PARIS — Les services de renseignement intérieur français vont rompre leur contrat avec l’entreprise technologique américaine Palantir, a annoncé mardi matin le Premier ministre. “La société française ChapsVision a été retenue aujourd’hui par la DGSI, la Direction générale de la sécurité intérieure, qui dépend du ministre de l’Intérieur, pour se substituer au géant américain Palantir”, a détaillé Sébastien Lecornu dans une vidéo publiée à la veille de l’ouverture du salon du numérique VivaTech. C

stories.title: Violent attacks on schools, pupils and staff around the world up by 40%, says study
stories.summary: Cases reported in 83 countries, with at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted or arrested, GCPEA says Attacks on education globally have surged by 40% with more than 8,556 recorded incidents and 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, arrested or otherwise harmed in 2024 and 2025, according to new research. Attacks were reported in 83 countries, with the highest incidences recorded in Colombia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Haiti, Palestine and

stories.title: Indian outrage over US killing of sailors mounts as leaders attend G7 summit
stories.summary: Relations at lowest ebb in years after Washington refuses to apologise for deaths in strait of Hormuz Fury has continued to mount in India over the US’s refusal to apologise for the deaths of Indian sailors killed in strikes in the strait of Hormuz, further straining relations between the two countries as their leaders meet at the G7 summit in France this week. Last week, three Indian seafarers, who were working on board commercial oil tankers, were killed when the US launched missile strikes on

stories.title: Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames
stories.summary: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was set on fire as Russia struck multiple cities in Ukraine overnight. Meanwhile, Moscow reported three deaths from a Ukrainian drone attack.

stories.title: Ukraine: Kyiv under fire again as russian strikes send residents into shelters
stories.summary: Ukraine's capital came under a major missile attack early on 15 June, with air defence systems intercepting projectiles above the city. Authorities reported injuries as residents sought refuge in shelters and metro stations.

stories.title: Canada eliminates human rights watchdog that oversees companies operating abroad
stories.summary: Mark Carney says Canadian Ombudsperson for Responsible Enterprise office hasn’t been ‘effective’ since its 2019 setup Canada is eliminating a watchdog that investigates alleged human rights violations committed by Canadian companies operating abroad, after Mark Carney said the office hadn’t been “effective” since it was set up in 2019. The move comes as Canada faces criticism from Donald Trump’s administration over its “unacceptable” efforts to combat forced labour. Continue reading...

stories.title: Oil prices hit three-month low and markets reach record high amid Iran deal breakthrough
stories.summary: Donald Trump posts ‘Let the oil flow’ as US-Iran peace deal sparks immediate drop for Brent crude Analysis: Oil and gas unlikely to return to prewar prices for months even if Hormuz reopens Global oil prices have tumbled to a three-month low and stock markets closed at a record high amid fresh hopes that a US-Iran peace deal could end the greatest energy supply crisis in the history of the market. The price of Brent crude dropped about 4% to about $83 (£62) on Monday amid optimism that the strai

stories.title: Bonnie Tyler out of coma but remains in intensive care in Portugal
stories.summary: Welsh singer, best known for 1983 hit Total Eclipse of the Heart, had emergency intestinal surgery in May Welsh pop star Bonnie Tyler is no longer in a coma but remains “very unwell” in intensive care at a hospital near her home in Faro, Portugal. The 75-year-old singer received emergency intestinal surgery in May and was placed in an induced coma to aid her recovery. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
stories.summary: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't end shipping risks
stories.summary: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But naval mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.

stories.title: UN calls for drone regulation in conflict zones
stories.summary: UN rights chief Volker Türk said more than 1,000 civilians in Sudan have been killed this year by drone attacks. The UN has called for regulation and accountability to prevent similar atrocities.

stories.title: Palestinian leader Abbas announces elections
stories.summary: Presidential and legislative elections haven't taken place in the Palestinian territories for twenty years due to war and occupation, with veteran President Mahmoud Abbas ruling by decree.

stories.title: UK announces social media ban for under-16s
stories.summary: The United Kingdom is set to follow the example of Australia and Indonesia by banning teenagers from social networks. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said platforms like Instagram and TikTok make youngsters "unhappy."

stories.title: Watch: What does the US-Iran deal to end war mean for Lebanon and Israel?
stories.summary: It's not clear if the deal means the Israelis will pause their offensive in Lebanon, BBC international editor Jeremy Bowen reports.

stories.title: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.title: From energy corridors to AI: Can the Turkic world become a new economic force?
stories.summary: Turkic states met in Shusha this weekend to boost competition law, digital cooperation and trade, aiming to align antitrust rules and strengthen regional connectivity.

stories.title: MSF staff abused Sudanese refugees in sex-for-food scandal
stories.summary: Some victims said they chose not to speak out because they feared staff would cut off access to aid.

stories.title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
stories.summary: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.

stories.title: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies after fall into volcanic crater
stories.summary: A Yemeni adventurer known online as the “Spider-Man of Yemen” died after falling into a volcanic crater while attempting a climb without safety equipment.

stories.title: Meet Merlin: The duck that became the World Cup's viral mascot
stories.summary: A duck named Merlin has become an unlikely World Cup sensation after videos of him wearing a Mexico jersey and joining celebrations in Mexico City spread across social media.

stories.title: 21-year-old woman dies after being launched off bridge without safety rope
stories.summary: Viral videos of the tragedy shared online show the anguish of onlookers when they realised the lady was launched off the Ponte do Esqueleto bridge in Brazil without the required safety rope. “She’s got no rope,” one witness to the accident is heard saying.

stories.title: 8 dead as B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards Air Force Base in California
stories.summary: A B-52 Stratofortress went down at around 11:20 a.m. local time on Monday, bursting into flames and killing everyone on board.

stories.title: 'Greatest feeling ever': Cape Verdeans tell BBC of joy at holding Spain to draw
stories.summary: The streets of the capital, Praia, shook with wild celebrations as the small island nation held Spain to a shock 0-0 draw.

stories.title: Muslera bakija után Szaúd-Arábia pontot szerzett Uruguay ellen a vb-n
stories.summary: A szaúdi kapus, Muhammed al-Ovaisz majdnem minden lövést és fejest kivédett.

stories.title: Irán–Új-Zéland 2-2
Nem sokkal felszállása után a földbe csapódott egy amerikai nehézbombázó Kaliforniában
stories.summary: A balesetről megjelent videók szerint a gép szinte teljesen megsemmisült.

stories.title: Jóval több parlamenti képviselő jut pénzhez ingatlan bérbeadásából, mint az országos átlag
stories.summary: Vannak arra nemzetközi példák, hogy az érdekeltség befolyásolhatja a törvényhozást, ezért lehet jelentősége, hogy itthon a képviselők tizede szerez jövedelmet ingatlan bérbeadásából.

stories.title: Egy rendőr orra előtt esett el az e-rollerrel: 1,8 millió forintos büntetést kapott és a jogsi is ugrott másfél évre
stories.summary: A szigorú büntetést tudomásul vette az ittasan vezető rolleres és családja, azon viszont már megütköztek, hogy az ügyész súlyosbításért fellebbezett. Ráadásul az e-rollert nem ismeri a KRESZ. De meddig marad ez így?

stories.title: Miért nincs elég szaporulat a struccfarmokon? Mert a madarak inkább a gondozóik iránt táplálnak gyengéd érzelmeket
stories.summary: 2002-ben Ignobel-díjat is nyert az a kutatás, ami kimutatta: a brit struccfarmokon azért van kevés bébistrucc, mert a madarakat jobban érdeklik az emberek, mint a saját fajtársaik, ezért kevesebbszer párosodnak egymással.

stories.title: Megölhettek egy idős nőt a VIII. kerületben, a rendőrség hajtóvadászatot indított
stories.summary: A hatóságok bejelentés alapján mentek ki egy Práter utcai lakáshoz.

stories.title: Elijah Just Do It: az új-zélandiak támadója két gólt lőtt Irán ellen a vb-n
stories.summary: Az egész vb-mezőny leggyengébbjének tartott új-zélandi válogatott döntetlennel kezdte a tornát.

stories.title: Netanjahu is megszólalt az USA és Irán közötti megállapodás után, azt mondta, nem mindig értenek egyet Trumppal
stories.summary: Az izraeli miniszterelnök egyúttal közölte, hogy Izrael nem szándékozik kivonulni Dél-Libanonból, Gázából vagy Szíriából.

stories.title: A FIFA megengedi Trumpnak, hogy a döntő után ő adja át a világbajnoki trófeát
stories.summary: Azt is az amerikai elnökre bízzák, hogy együtt akar-e ünnepelni a dobogón a győztes csapat tagjaival.

stories.title: Prefektusa vágta gyomorszájon a diákot az esztergomi ferences kollégiumban, ahol a pedagógiai eszköztár része a megalázó büntetés
stories.summary: A Szent Antal Ferences Gimnázium és Kollégium nevelője elismerte a Telexnek, hogy elvesztette az önuralmát. Állítása szerint azért ütötte meg a 15 éves fiút, mert a gyerek nem ment el zuhanyozni és provokálta őt. A rendőrség könnyű testi sértés miatt ismeretlen tettes ellen kezdett nyomozni, de a jogszabályok miatt csekély a mozgástere.

stories.title: Pintér Bence örülne, ha Győrben lenne a Nemzeti Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatal székhelye
stories.summary: A győri polgármester az előrehozott önkormányzati választást országos szinten nem támogatná.

stories.title: Jön a kánikula, de kedden még okozhat meglepetést az időjárás
stories.summary: Zivatarok miatt adtak ki figyelmeztetést.

stories.title: Még hetekbe telhet, mire megkezdik a hajók az átkelést a Hormuzi-szoroson
stories.summary: A világ legnagyobb tankerhajó-üzemeltetőjének vezetője arról beszélt a Financial Timesnak, hogy meg kell győződniük róla, hogy érdemi megállapodás történt a felek között.

stories.title: Trump pénteken teheti közzé az Iránnal kötött megállapodást
stories.summary: J. D. Vance alelnök szerint egy másfél oldalas, nagyon általános dokumentumról van szó.

stories.title: Rubovszky Rita: Fontos belenéznünk a tükörbe, mennyire vált az egyházi iskola a társadalmi szegregáció eszközévé
stories.summary: A Ciszterci Iskolai Főhatóság igazgatója szerint az oktatást nem lehet fűnyíróelv alapján finanszírozni.

stories.title: Hegedűs minisztériuma módosítaná a titkos örökbefogadáshoz kötődő egészségügyi adatkezelést
stories.summary: Nem szakadna ketté az örökbefogadott gyerek egészségügyi dokumentációja a családhoz kerüléssel.

stories.title: A Los Angeles-i vb-meccs lefújása után rögtön visszaküldték Mexikóba az iráni válogatottat
stories.summary: Amír Galenoji szövetségi kapitány és több játékos is a világbajnokság szervezőire panaszkodik.

stories.title: Újabb nyomozást rendeltek el a pécsi Volvo-botrány ügyében Hadházy Ákos szerint
stories.summary: A volt képviselőt szóban tájékoztatták, hogy megindult a feljelentése alapján a nyomozás.

stories.title: Arte: Mennyire jogszerű harmadik országokba toloncolni a menedékkérőket?
stories.summary: Az EU zöld utat adott a tagállamoknak az úgynevezett „visszatérési központokra”.

stories.title: Magánorvos pár száz forintért? Erre jó az egészségpénztárak olcsó biztosítása
stories.summary: Robbanásszerű növekedésnek indult a hazai magánegészségügy, a lakosság egyre mélyebben nyúl a zsebébe, ha minőségi és gyors ellátást szeretne. A gyorsaságnak és a prémium szolgáltatásoknak azonban komoly ára van, a vizsgálati és diagnosztikai díjak sokszor több százezer forintot is elvihetnek a családi kasszából. Az egészségpénztári tagok azonban hozzáférhetnek olyan magánegészségügyi szolgáltatásokhoz is, amelyek milliós pénzügyi védőhálót és azonnali ellátásszervezést nyújtanak a legsúlyosabb

stories.title: Egy francia kisvárosban üléseznek a G7-országok vezetői, találkozik Trump, Zelenszkij és Ursula von der Leyen
stories.summary: Evian-les-Bains városában üléseznek az állami vezetők, ahol arról is meg akarják győzni Trumpot: a G7-nek érdemi tárgyalásokat kell kezdenie Putyinnal.

stories.title: Négy éve az argentinokat verte a vb-n, most Tunéziával kellene csodát tennie az Afrika-kupa-győztes edzőnek
stories.summary: A francia Hervé Renard ugrik be tunéziai kapitánynak, öt napja lesz felrázni az öt gólt kapó csapatot.

stories.title: A vasúti fuvarozás versenyképessége a tét (x)
stories.summary: Előnyös lehet a vasúti egyeskocsi-fuvarozás állami támogatásának visszaállítása – új feltételekkel.

stories.title: Eddig ismeretlen Bartók-mű kézirata került elő
stories.summary: A spanyolországi árverésen hibásan albumlapként szereplő tétel páratlan értékkel gazdagítja a Bartók repertoárt.

stories.title: Nyomozás indul, miután feljelentést tett a győri alpolgármester, mert több helyi szervezet is kapott NKA-s pénzt
stories.summary: Kósa Roland négy helyi egyesületről írta, hogy szerintük furcsa körülmények között működtek, és azt is, hogy mindegyiknek köze van Juhász Balázs abdai fideszes önkormányzati képviselőhöz.

stories.title: Previously unknown Bartók manuscript discovered
stories.summary: The exceptionally valuable manuscript surfaced at an auction in Spain, and there is no doubt as to its authenticity.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid: Szerdától hétköznap és vasárnap is látogatható a Karmelita ezen a héten
stories.summary: A miniszter szerint még bőven tart az érdeklődés a látogatás iránt.

stories.title: Tuchel pár éve szörnyűnek nevezte azt a francia bírót, aki most az angolok első meccsét fogja vezetni a vb-n
stories.summary: A német edző nincs túl jó barátságban Clément Turpinnel.

stories.title: Az Amnesty és a Helsinki szerint is szakítania kellene a Tiszának a Fidesz-kormány migránsozásával
stories.summary: A jogvédő szervezetek szerint változtatni kellene azon a kirekesztő nyelven, amit a korábbi politikai vezetés használt.

stories.title: Trump a G7-csúcson: Iránnak sosem lesz atomfegyvere a megállapodás szerint
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök szerint korrekt az Iránnal kötött megállapodás.

stories.title: Elbocsátják a Pesti Srácok összes munkavállalóját
stories.summary: Huth Gergely szerint ez még nem azt jelenti, hogy a lap is megszűnik.

stories.title: Mother hopes to get justice for murdered daughter in retrial in Dominican Republic
stories.summary: Shirley Firth is hoping those responsible for Lindsay de Feliz’s death in 2019 will finally be convicted A Cambridgeshire mother in her 90s is hoping to finally see justice for her murdered daughter when a retrial into her death is due to open in the Dominican Republic this week. The body of Lindsay de Feliz, 64, a successful author, was found in a shallow grave, close to her home in the north-west of the Dominican Republic, in December 2019. Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe is starting to break up with US big tech. But it’s still abiding by the Silicon Valley rulebook | Max von Thun
stories.summary: The European Commission has unveiled its plans for digital sovereignty. Its proposals betray a disappointing lack of vision Beti Hohler is a Slovenian national who lives in the Netherlands. Like tens of millions of other Europeans, she uses Apple’s app store and has an Amazon account. When she travels for work or leisure, she may want to book a place on Airbnb or Booking, using a credit card issued by Visa or Mastercard, perhaps through PayPal. But when the Trump administration sanctioned her la

stories.title: Singer Oliver Tree and YouTuber Gaspi among six killed as two helicopters collide in Brazil
stories.summary: Brazil's air force has launched an investigation into the incident, which took place over Rio de Janeiro.

stories.title: Police mistakenly shoot and kill Australian child in eastern Pakistan, officials say
stories.summary: Australian media reported the family, from the western city of Perth, were visiting relatives in Pakistan when the incident took place.

stories.title: Brazil woman dies after rope-jumping instructors fail to attach cord
stories.summary: Three men have been arrested after instructors failed to attach a rope to her before helping her jump from a bridge.

stories.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres
stories.summary: ‘Coastal uplift’ exposes coral and kills marine life, as residents say shorelines extended by up to 200 metres A powerful earthquake that killed at least 61 people in the Philippines this week raised the seabed by as much as 2 metres (6.6ft), exposing coral and harming marine life, the country’s environment department has said. At least 40 people are still missing after the 7.8-magnitude quake off southern Mindanao island on Monday, according to updated tolls from the disaster agency. Continue r

stories.title: Weather tracker: Saharan heat to send temperatures soaring across Europe
stories.summary: Heatwave conditions build over much of continent, while mild start to winter continues in parts of Australia Hot weather is expected across Europe this week as heatwave conditions build over large swathes of the continent. A mass of hot air from the Sahara has settled over the Iberian peninsula and spread into southern and western France, pushing temperatures widely into the low- and mid-30s celsius. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tényleg volt egy Hitler-hasonmás a német szurkolók között a Curaçao elleni vb-meccsen?
stories.summary: 1,4 millió megtekintésnél járt az X-en megosztott fotó, amin a jellegzetes bajuszt és frizurát viselő német szurkoló szerepel.

stories.title: Lukaku beállt és megmentette Belgiumot Egyiptom ellen
stories.summary: A 2018-ban bronzérmes Belgium hátrányba került Egyiptommal szemben, szerencséje volt, hogy nem kapta meg a második gólt is. Lukaku beállása után viszont könnyen fordíthatott volna. Élvezetes meccsen alakult ki az 1–1-es döntetlen.

stories.title: Szaúd-Arábia–Uruguay 1-1
Swiss voters reject population cap proposal
stories.summary: A proposal to introduce a population cap of 10 million in the country has failed. It was put forward by a hard-right party.

stories.title: Germany start 2026 World Cup with win over Curacao
stories.summary: For the first time since they won the tournament in 2014, Germany won their opening game at the World Cup.

stories.title: Manuel Neuer: The aura of a record-breaking goalkeeper
stories.summary: Germany's hopes at the 2026 World Cup are connected to world-class goalkeeper Manuel Neuer's fitness and form.

stories.title: How geothermal energy could power US homes
stories.summary: Advancements in geothermal systems and sustained political support in the US provide an opportunity for this clean energy industry to scale.

stories.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
stories.summary: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

stories.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?
stories.summary: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.

stories.title: Iran warns of 'imminent' response as Israel strikes Beirut
stories.summary: Iran warns of imminent retaliation after fresh Israeli strikes kill five in southern Lebanon, as Trump publicly slams Netanyahu over the timing.

stories.title: Everything you need to know ahead of the White House's controversial UFC 250 event
stories.summary: The Freedom UFC 250 event will celebrate the United States' 250th anniversary as well as US President Donald Trump's 80th birthday.

stories.title: Dutch supporters bring sea of orange to World Cup host city
stories.summary: Thousands of Dutch fans dressed in orange marched, danced and chanted outside Dallas Stadium ahead of the Netherlands' World Cup match against Japan. A handful of Japanese supporters also joined the festive pre-match atmosphere.

stories.title: Germany cruises past Curaçao 7-1 and Netherlands-Japan tie 2-2 as World Cup group stages continue
stories.summary: Curaçao made history on Sunday after they scored their first goal in the World Cup in their first ever participation in the tournament, despite suffering a 7-1 loss to European giants and former world champions, Germany.

stories.title: Trump celebrates 80th birthday with Iran deal and UFC cage fight on the White House South Lawn
stories.summary: Trump delayed his attendance to the French region of Evian where leaders of the Group of Seven – or G7 – are set to meet to host his UFC fight birthday event.

stories.title: EU leaders welcome US-Iran deal to end war as Macron says mission to aid Hormuz reopening 'ready'
stories.summary: The US and Iran said they had reached a deal to end the war on all fronts, including Lebanon, and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz, though they offered few details about Tehran's nuclear programme.

stories.title: Switzerland: Clashes in Geneva as 20,000 protest G7 summit and global policies
stories.summary: Clashes broke out in Geneva on 14 June after a large demonstration held ahead of the G7 summit in nearby Évian-les-Bains, France, turned violent near the United Nations district.

stories.title: World Naked Bike Ride returns to streets of London in the UK
stories.summary: Hundreds of cyclists took part in the World Naked Bike Ride in London on 14 June. The annual event combined humour, environmental messages, and calls for greater safety on the roads.

stories.title: Starbucks to close outlets across Korea for history lesson after 'Tank Day' ad fiasco
stories.summary: The day of the reusable cup promotion coincided with the anniversary of the Gwangju uprising in which 165 civilians were killed, though many believe the real figure to be much higher.

stories.title: At least 2,300 killed this year in Haiti gang violence, UN human rights chief says
stories.summary: Haiti has been plagued by worsening gang violence since the 2021 assassination of its then-President Jovenel Moïse.

stories.title: Russian attack sets fire to a UNESCO-listed monastery in Kyiv
stories.summary: A major Russian attack sparked a fire at a Kyiv monastery, as missiles and drones hit multiple sites across the Ukrainian capital.

stories.title: Iran declares victory and Israel holds its ground as US framework deal faces first test
stories.summary: Tehran declared the US "was forced to sign" a surrender while Iran's military said enemies had "no option but to accept defeat," as Israel said IDF forces would remain in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza "for an unlimited period".

stories.title: Delhi issues ‘strong protest’ after US strikes kill three Indian seafarers in Gulf
stories.summary: Washington claims vessel was violating its blockade of Iranian ports and failed to comply with instructions The Indian government has voiced a “strong protest” after three Indian seafarers were killed in US military strikes against oil tankers travelling through the strait of Hormuz. US Central Command confirmed that its aircraft had fired two Hellfire missiles at the engine room of the MT Settebello as it sailed through the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany faces EU penalty over gender pay gap directive
stories.summary: Germany was required to incorporate the EU Pay Transparency Directive into its national law, but it has missed the deadline.

stories.title: Gold rush atmosphere at Berlin's military air trade show
stories.summary: This year's ILA Berlin Air Show is breaking records, primarily because of the booming defense sector, which is reaping billions in contracts as a result of wars and crises.

stories.title: The creatives trying to fix Germany's bureaucracy
stories.summary: Berlin played host to the 9th annual Creative Bureaucracy Festival this week, where highly-motivated public service workers explained why a healthy democracy needs a functioning state.

stories.title: Germany news: President Steinmeier heads to Asia
stories.summary: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier is kicking off a trip to Asia. And veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer will take to the field with the national squad in Germany's first match of the 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Manuel Neuer fit to join Germany in first World Cup match
stories.summary: Veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer is set to play in Germany's first match in this year's World Cup. He had retired from the national team in August 2024.

stories.title: Iran: officials announce dates for Ali Khamenei funeral and burial
stories.summary: State media in Iran reported on Saturday, June 13, that the funeral and burial of Ali Khamenei, former leader of the Islamic Republic, will be held from July 4 to July 9, 2026

stories.title: 'Wake-up call': Europe reacts to Anthropic halting access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models
stories.summary: Anthropic said it believed the US government had become aware of a potential means of jailbreaking Fable 5.

stories.title: New York erupts as Knicks win first NBA title since 1973
stories.summary: Knicks fans flooded the streets of New York after the team defeated the Spurs 94-90 to claim its first NBA championship in more than five decades.

stories.title: Trump says US-Iran peace deal 'complete,' to be signed on Friday
stories.summary: Tehran has since confirmed that the "text of the memorandum of understanding" has been agreed on and is to be signed in Switzerland, opening the door to 60-day negotiations "following verification of US commitments," including the lifting of the US blockade.

stories.title: US diplomat found dead in Myanmar as police treat case as possible homicide
stories.summary: Thai woman is in custody in connection with investigation after man was reportedly found dead at a hotel in Yangon A US diplomat has been found dead in Myanmar ’s largest city, and members of the diplomatic community in Yangon say a Thai woman has been detained by police in connection with the investigation. US officials in Thailand and the US embassy in Myanmar referred questions on the case to the state department, which confirmed the “death of a US government employee” assigned to the embassy

stories.title: What Albania teaches us about Kushner's real estate tactics – video explainer
stories.summary: Jared Kushner, Donald Trump’s son-in-law, has finalized a deal with Albanian officials to allow construction on Sazan Island in the Adriatic Sea. The $1.6bn project is expected to build luxury tourism real estate on the pristine island and surrounding waters and wetlands. Spurned by the potential environmental impact of the plan and the possibility of corrupt dealings, demonstrators have taken to the streets of Albania's capital city demanding an end to the project. Cate Brown, the Guardian's po

stories.title: London council seizes social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone first lady
stories.summary: Fatima Jabbe-Bio kept tenancy in Southwark despite living for much of year at presidential lodge in Freetown A social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone’s first lady has been seized by a London council. Southwark council confirmed it had repossessed the two-bedroom home in Walworth previously occupied by Fatima Jabbe-Bio, whose tenancy was reported by the Times last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: Up to 90% of Ireland’s asylum seekers may have entered from Northern Ireland, data shows
stories.summary: Figures suggest common travel area being used in both directions, but particularly UK to Ireland Up to 90% of asylum seekers in Ireland may have entered the country via the Northern Ireland land border in the last three years, figures suggest. Irish government data shows the common travel area (CTA) is being exploited in both directions but suggests it may be more popular for those seeking asylum in Ireland than in the UK. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK: Defense Secretary John Healey quits in military spending dispute
stories.summary: Britain's defense minister has abruptly quit his post, saying the government's plans to bolster defense spending in the coming years are inadequate. The move puts additional pressure on embattled UK PM Keir Starmer.

stories.title: German students up in arms about funding cuts
stories.summary: Germany's research minister, Dorothee Bär, says the situation of German students is "very privileged" and has rejected reform of the Federal Training Assistance Act (BAföG), thereby turning many students against her.
